lessonTitle: Evidence-Based Approaches to Support Transitions for AYAs with SCD

items.text: Review a variety of resources to aid in the transition of adolescents to adult care.
Discuss health living strategies that must also be a priority, to help keep patients as holistically healthy as they can be.
Identify strategies for being successful at school and work while managing the complexities of SCD.

data.html: Now that we have a better foundation on some transition challenges and enables, let's review specific resources for transitioning AYAs.
Knowledge Check Determine whether the following statements are true or false.

data.question: Motivated young individuals with good social support networks and supportive families tend to transition successfully.
data.feedback_correct: True. Motivated young individuals with good social support networks and supportive families tend to transition successfully.

data.question: Trusting relationships and open communication between patients and paediatric providers contribute to successful transitions.
data.feedback_correct: True. Trusting relationships and open communication between patients and paediatric providers contribute to successful transitions.

data.question: Social and family support plays a minor role in facilitating the transition process.
data.feedback_correct: False. Social and family support plays a crucial role in facilitating the transition process.

slides.title: Managing SCD in School or Work

blocks.title: Managing SCD in School or Work

data.html:
Managing SCD in School or Work

If youth are moving to a different area for studies/employment, encourage them to find a healthcare provider, preferably with knowledge and experience with treating people with SCD.

Provide student health services the most up-to-date medical records before starting a school year. This is important in case of an emergency.

Include the types of accommodation youth may need:

items.html:
Accommodation during extreme hot or cold weather:
  • Allowing for water to be kept at desk/nearby
  • Allowing for layered clothing
  • Avoiding physical activities in extreme weather conditions

Special care of injuries
  • Never apply a cold pack to an injury or pain site

data.html: To learn more information on post secondary school accommodations, check out the University Health Network Red Blood Cell Disorders Blog https://www.rbcd.ca/blog/accessibility-services-rbcd

blocks.title: Is the following statement true or false?

data.question: Hold private appointments solely with the parent/s of AYAs during their transition into adulthood.
data.feedback_correct: This statement is false. Include youth in the conversation about their comprehensive care plan. You may also schedule a private appointment without the parent/s to help them feel more comfortable with expressing their needs.

data.title: Community Resources
data.body: Consider referring patients to community supports such as those provided through SCAGO: https://sicklecellanemia.ca/transitioning-from-pediatric-to-adult-care, which provides supports like school-preparedness seminars and peer support groups.

items.text: There are numerous barriers to transition including adolescent self-management skill set, previous negative experiences in the emergency department, neurocognitive deficits due to stroke, sociodemographic factors, and psychosocial/emotional status.
The stigma around accommodations at school or work may make patients/families hesitant to speak about it. Help dispel myths, so they can be informed about resources that will benefit them.
Incorporate a variety of ways to collaborate with your patient for success. Problem solving education, disease specific education, and music therapy are a few examples.
